pipeline.title: Pipeline Builder
pipeline.primary_workflow: Governed Build (?pane=build) — a resumable, wallet-gated MaterializingRun ladder over EXISTING daemon authority. Stages: Review (all 7 ladder rungs re-read from daemon truth, coherence-gated) -> Admit materializing run (POST /odk/materializing-runs, receipt) -> Request lease (403 wallet challenge -> externally signed grant -> POST :id/acquire-lease, receipt) -> Admit connector session (POST /odk/connector-sessions, receipt) -> Open sealed session (POST :id/open, credential resolved server-side, receipt) -> Execute ONE bounded read (POST :id/execute, materialized_output_registered receipt) -> Registered (a materialized-object-set with real rows + pre_output_receipt_ref, linked to Preview/Explorer/Lineage/Vertex/Provenance). Every stage is a declared receipted action; the STAGE is a pure function of record status re-read on every render, so refresh resumes exactly and a stale/foreign id fails closed to review. Confirmed live: ont pipe-parity reaches Preview rows L-1/L-2/L-3 (First/Second/Third Loan) citing mset_18c15c5d9d5bfc0f / mrun_18c15c5cd7095669 / csn_18c15c5d306798fa.

pipeline.security_credential_implications: The Build workflow crosses REAL authority and is the estate's most credential-sensitive operational path — audited as correctly fail-closed. A wallet_approval_grant is pasted from an EXTERNAL wallet, parsed as bounded JSON, forwarded to the daemon exactly ONCE over a single opaque field (fieldMax 8192), and never persisted, logged, or echoed; the grant blob exists only for the one forward POST. A 403 challenge is returned as a typed refusal whose redirect carries ONLY the two public commitment hashes (policy_hash/request_hash, sha256:-shape validated) — the signed grant itself never appears in any URL. The sealed ConnectorSession resolves credential material SERVER-SIDE (plan boundary lease.credential_material=false; a 428 credential_unresolved refusal fires if no resolvable sealed credential exists). Credential material NEVER renders: datasource endpoints are reduced to origin with path redacted; the lease/session inspector shows status booleans + refs only. Confused-deputy is fail-closed: the daemon re-checks connector-covers-source-origin at every stage. Execute is exactly ONE bounded read (row limit clamped 1-500, redirects refused, all-or-nothing validation). The serve holds NO signer (a dev test signer exists only under an explicit flag). Success is refused if the daemon returns no receipt_ref (receipt_missing fail-closed). No new credential surface is introduced by this surface — it consumes existing sealed-credential + wallet-grant primitives.

The reference's real intent-to-result WORKFLOW, however, is New source -> select source type -> configure connection -> DECLARE a source (a durable record). RESOLVED in the held stack (#69, open + held for review; not yet merged to master): the declare half is implemented — sources is a bound module (surfaces/sources) declaring ONE receipted mutation; New source and Connect-to-external-system open the governed declare pane; the daemon POST is atomic (record-first/receipt-second with checked rollback, typed 5xx lanes) and returns the dsr_ receipt explicitly; operational_state promoted browse -> act with capability create. sources.security_credential_implications: The declare cut touches the credential boundary but NEVER a secret. POST /v1/hypervisor/data-sources is already fail-closed: it rejects plaintext-secret keys (data_source_plaintext_secret_rejected) and credential-bearing endpoints/userinfo/sensitive query params (data_source_endpoint_credentialed — verified in the daemon handler), and accepts only a declared credential_posture (a posture string from CREDENTIAL_POSTURES, never a value). The surface renders endpoints scheme+host+path only (userinfo/query/fragment stripped) and states that credential VALUES never appear in the surface, the records, or receipts. The durable result of the cut is a declared registry record + a dsr_ receipt — no wallet, no capability lease, no live connection, no extraction, no spend, so there is no irreversible or credential/wallet risk in scope. The only new obligations the PR must uphold: the declare FORM must expose NO free-text secret field (posture picker only), and the endpoint sanitization must be preserved on both submit and render. The genuinely credential/wallet-risky step — the extraction/live-connection authority crossing — stays OUT of scope, disabled with its named gap.

schema.security_credential_implications: Schema authoring is already governed and LOW-risk: create/patch run server-side (browser never submits a canonical_object_model — the daemon clones the current COM and applies ONE bounded structured edit), guarded by optimistic concurrency (expected_revision → 409 refusal with state untouched), every success carries a durable ontology_receipt, and definition ids are immutable after create so edits are non-destructive-by-construction and reversible via new revisions. explorer.landing_vs_workflow_note: The IOI certified shell is the Object Explorer LANDING (search hero + shortcuts + object-type catalog + object-set catalog) — pixel-frozen chrome over real ODK truth. The reference's real intent-to-result path runs PAST that landing: select an object type → /exploration workspace → filter object INSTANCES → save as a List/object set. IOI implements the READ half of this path with genuine depth (select → semantic inspector → materialized-set provenance + real row preview + cross-app deep links) but NOT the write/browse half — object-instance search, exploration/list authoring, and action execution are all named gaps with no daemon plane. POSITIVE CONTROL (pipeline control-matrix.mjs, 4→6 mapping): daemon_action→daemon_read for read-nav bindings (node-select ?node=, lineage, manager, add-data, warnings, picker) and →governed_receipted_action for cmd.build (?pane=build) + the preview/materialized tabs; local_view→local_view_interaction; disabled_reason→disabled_missing_authority; unsupported→unsupported_reference_session (tabs-mode/branch/proposal/undo/favorites) with a couple reference_data_only (batch badge). Pipeline's ?pane=build IS a confirmed intent-to-durable-result journey (MaterializingRun → wallet-approved lease → sealed ConnectorSession → one bounded execute → receipts, resumable from record status) — which is precisely the governed-action shape Explorer's recommended PR borrows, without duplicating Build.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.primary_workflow: Decide an approval request: filter/select a pending request in the inbox -> inspect its record (subject, request id, reason, blast radius, created) -> approve (optional reviewer_ref, no confirm) / reject (confirm) / revoke (confirm, on approved) through the governed PATCH transition, which returns a durable approval-transition receipt and lands a typed success/refusal banner linked to the /__ioi/work-ledger proof stream. NOT a create surface — requests are minted by other flows; the inbox is the review-and-decide plane.

status-multiselect-popover (9 states: Open/Approved/Changes-requested/Action-required/Pending-approval/Draft/Closed/Rejected-and-closed/Completed — WORKS in capture)
request-type-multiselect-popover (8 kinds: Access/Add-reference/Ontology-proposals/Workshop-proposals/Deletion/CDM-Publish/Retention-policy/User-intake — WORKS)
created-by-user-picker (opens but data-gated/empty)
sort-menu (Recently created / Oldest created — WORKS)
clear-filters + workspace-sidebar-nav hover

NOT REACHED IN REFERENCE: request detail + approve/reject action — the request LIST is data-gated (dead XHR -> 'No requests found'), so the decide workflow is unreachable in the mirror capture; only IOI (with 9 real daemon records) reaches detail + governed transitions
approvals.landing_vs_workflow_note: The reference landing IS the inbox (faceted filters + request list), not a splash/onboarding screen. The PRIMARY WORKFLOW is decide-a-request: select a pending row -> inspect detail -> approve/reject/revoke. In the reference mirror the request list is data-gated ('No requests found.'), so the detail/action states are UNREACHABLE in the reference capture — but the IOI surface has real daemon data (9 requests: 8 pending verify_rebind + 1 approved improvement-proposal, all cross-checked against GET /v1/hypervisor/governance/approval-requests) and fully reaches detail + the governed approve/reject/revoke transitions with durable receipts. So IOI goes FURTHER than the reference capture can. There is no create/new-request button in either surface — approvals is a review plane, not an authoring plane. POSITIVE CONTROL (pipeline): pipeline/control-matrix.mjs 4 outcomes map to the 6-taxonomy as daemon_action->{daemon_read for read navigation (node-select ?node=, warnings tab, lineage links) + governed_receipted_action for cmd.build}, local_view->local_view_interaction, disabled_reason->disabled_missing_authority, unsupported->{unsupported_reference_session for branch/proposal/version/undo/redo + reference_data_only for certified chrome like favorite-star/batch-badge}; ?pane=build IS a real intent-to-durable-result journey (the governed ODK ladder: MaterializingRun -> wallet-approved lease [403 challenge -> externally-signed grant] -> sealed ConnectorSession -> ONE bounded execute, each stage a declared receipted action, resumable from record status) — confirmed via the cmd.build proof text in control-matrix.mjs.

approvals.security_credential_implications: The act workflow crosses GOVERNANCE authority only — approve/reject/revoke are receipted state transitions; no credential, wallet, secret, or irreversible-deletion surface is involved. Safety properties already in place: (1) fail-closed receipts — a 200 without transition_receipt.receipt_ref matching schema_version ioi.hypervisor.governance.approval-transition-receipt.v1 is downgraded to a failure, so a mutation without proof is never trusted; (2) confirmation posture — reject/revoke require an explicit confirm checkbox that the SERVER re-enforces (confirmation_required typed refusal), approve submits directly; (3) allowlisted fields — only reviewer_ref is forwarded (bounded 2000 chars); (4) same-origin bounded return path (safeReturnPath) — the module never controls the raw redirect. Residual truthful gaps: reviewer_ref is unauthenticated free-text attribution (no identity plane binds it to a principal); revoke (approved->revoked) is the most consequential transition but is confirm-gated + receipted. No new credential/wallet exposure is introduced by the recommended cut.

incidents.security_credential_implications: The incidents surface itself is read-only and exposes no credential or wallet secret; the embed render is single-rail with the og-grail element structurally stripped (verified 0 elements). The only EXISTING mutations that could back an operational cut carry real authority: POST /goal-runs/:id/start is WALLET-GATED and launches real compute (spend, semi-irreversible resource consumption); POST /goal-runs/:id/reconcile ADMITS reconciliation into the session workspace (mutates run/admission state). Any action must route through the #62 handleAction governance path so the wallet gate + durable receipt are enforced by the daemon, never synthesized in the UI. No credential input or irreversible destructive delete is in scope. The higher-risk provider families — GET/POST around /v1/hypervisor/incidents (provider-failure), POST /artifact-availability-incidents, and failover/run (real provider failover, spend/irreversible) — must stay OUT of this Missions surface (they belong to Operations); pulling them in would raise authority_security_risk from 3 to 4-5.

models.security_credential_implications: Low risk for the recommended cut because it introduces NO new authority — it re-hosts existing daemon mutations. probe performs a live upstream availability call (host_presence / ollama_tags) — read-side, no state mutation beyond persisting the probe result + receipt. enable/disable are reversible lifecycle transitions on a relaxed/admitted lane. select-default carries an exactly-one-default invariant that the daemon guards against concurrent winners. All four are planner-admitted (model-route-mutation-admission) and mint durable agentgres://model-route-receipt/* receipts with an op-transcript state_root. NO wallet signature, NO credential sealing, NO irreversible action is required for the seeded route (credential_posture=no_credentials_required). Credential custody stays untouched: the daemon rejects plaintext secrets (model_route_plaintext_secret_rejected) and only supports env-key posture, so BYOK/credentialed routes are out of scope of the cut. If create/patch/delete were later added to the surface, delete becomes the only semi-destructive lane and would warrant a confirm + receipt gate.

listings.landing_vs_workflow_note: The listings surface is a LANDING STOREFRONT, not a workflow surface. Its post-landing depth is a single daemon-backed store row + a display-only install wizard; there is no product catalog, no per-listing detail, and no action on the surface itself — the row merely links out to the owner substrate. The reference's actual primary action (Install, and its Upload sibling) is a MISSING-AUTHORITY gap in IOI (no install/draft/job route, no product-package upload route; publish != install). The estate's genuine intent-to-durable-result workflow — the governed publish ladder (draft listing -> publish candidate -> admission review -> POST /publish-candidates/:id/publish -> runtime-backed marketplace-publish-receipt) — already exists and is fully wired, but on the SIBLING owner surface /__ioi/marketplace, not here. POSITIVE-CONTROL CHECK (pipeline): pipeline/control-matrix.mjs uses a 4-outcome vocab that maps cleanly onto the 6-outcome taxonomy — daemon_action splits into daemon_read (read navigation, e.g. canvas.node-select, tray.tab-preview) and governed_receipted_action (cmd.build's ?pane=build ladder: MaterializingRun -> wallet-approved lease -> sealed ConnectorSession -> one bounded execute, each a declared receipted stage); local_view -> local_view_interaction; disabled_reason -> disabled_missing_authority; unsupported -> unsupported_reference_session (branch/proposal/favorites/undo) or reference_data_only (batch/org badge chrome). Pipeline's ?pane=build is confirmed a real intent-to-durable-result journey (a resumable governed ladder terminating in receipts) — exactly the kind of governed_receipted_action this listings browse surface LACKS, and which its costly install/upload parity would require. The listings surface has no governed_receipted_action of its own; its only real binding is the single daemon_read (GET /marketplace/listings).

listings.security_credential_implications: The recommended next cut (render published listings as a real product catalog + client-side search over the loaded set) is READ-ONLY over already-governed data (GET /marketplace/listings, filtered public_state=published) — no credential, wallet, or irreversible authority is touched, and drafts stay unlisted. Risk is confined to the DEFERRED reference parity: (1) the product-package UPLOAD lane ingests external artifacts/code -> supply-chain / code-execution exposure, must be sealed + governed + wallet-gated; (2) the INSTALL lane instantiates a product into a live workspace -> irreversible-ish runtime side effects, needs the governance ladder; (3) remote-marketplace federation introduces external trust boundaries. Publish itself is already fail-closed and receipted (requires admitted review + open ReleaseControl + mounted&serving runtime; emits marketplace-publish-receipt). So the operational-depth cut adds zero new credential exposure; the costly/risky lanes are explicitly named gaps, not built.

machinery.security_credential_implications: The recommended operational cut (structured definition authoring: create/edit/delete) crosses NO credential/wallet/external-authority boundary. State-machine records are inert LOCAL definitions guarded by typed server-side validation (17+ fail-closed codes); nothing seals a credential, acquires a capability lease, or contacts an external provider. The only irreversible op is DELETE, mitigated by a confirm gate + the durable history[] chain on siblings. This is the LOW-risk end of the estate — sharply unlike pipeline's Build ladder (wallet-approved lease → sealed ConnectorSession → bounded execute) or any provider/connector cut. Risk would rise ONLY if the (out-of-scope, missing) run/step/execute engine were built, since executing a process could carry real side effects — but that is a separate future contract, not this cut.

monitors.security_credential_implications: Moderate, no wallet/credential crossing for the recommended cut. Manual run (POST /automations/{id}/runs) is genuinely side-effecting — it executes the automation's declared command/agent steps over a real environment — but it is already bounded by the record's declared executor_identity + limits (per_exec_seconds 600, max_total 100) and produces a tamper-evident execution transcript, so it is a governed, receipted, replayable action rather than an opaque one. Pause/resume (PATCH enabled) is fully reversible and low risk. No secret is minted or exposed by run/pause. Credential-adjacent lanes exist but are OUT of the recommended scope: webhook-rotate touches webhook_token_hash, and DELETE is irreversible destruction. Keep those two off the monitors surface (they live on the substrate). Net: binding run + pause/resume adds real authority to a browse surface but stays inside existing, bounded, reversible, receipted contracts.

changes.title: Upgrade Assistant
changes.primary_workflow: Review-and-act on a pending upgrade. The reference lists platform "upgrades" grouped Pre-published / Published, each row carrying a "N of N pending" action in the MY ACTIONS / PENDING ACTIONS column (e.g. "Confirm or override the new default secure streaming preview setting" — an Admin action). The intent-to-result path is: operator reviews the change, then completes its pending action (confirm / override / opt-out / restrict) before a cutoff — a governed decision that mutates the upgrade's state. On the IOI plane this maps 1:1 to the improvement-proposal governance lane: review a proposal, then approve/reject (pending→approved/rejected) and apply (approved→applied), gated by fresh-simulation + approval + release. That mutation authority already exists on the daemon; the changes surface currently renders it read-only and delegates the action to Agent Studio.

Filters sidebar exercised (UPGRADE PROGRESS radios, UPGRADE TYPE checkboxes, SORT radios visible; facet counts live)

changes.landing_vs_workflow_note: The landing shell IS the change-inbox (grouped upgrade list) and is fully certified + faithfully ported read-only. It is NOT the workflow. The real intent-to-result path is one level deeper: the per-row 'N of N pending' action (MY ACTIONS / PENDING ACTIONS column) — review an upgrade, then confirm/override/opt-out before a cutoff, which mutates the upgrade's state. In the live capture that action path is data-gated (XHR 404s), but it is unambiguously the app's primary intent, not a splash/onboarding state (the What's-New modal is onboarding chrome, dismissed). On the IOI plane it maps 1:1 to the improvement-proposal governance transitions (approve/reject/apply/simulate), whose daemon authority + receipts already exist — so the operational cut is to surface that governed, receipted action on the row, advancing browse→act, rather than continuing to delegate it entirely to Agent Studio.

evalsuites.security_credential_implications: The recommended create/patch/delete cut touches NO credentials, wallet, or irreversible external effects — eval-suite records are local, INERT declarations (nothing runs, scores, or judges). Two daemon-side guardrails already fail-closed and MUST be surfaced as typed daemon errors (never bypassed client-side): (1) consent_requirements is a gate — a suite requiring only never_train evidence is rejected (eval_suite_consent_requirement_unusable), so the create authority cannot mint a suite that would assess unconsented data; (2) candidate_refs are allowlisted local schemes only (eval://, feedback://, training-candidate://) — external URLs are rejected (eval_suite_candidate_ref_invalid), closing a data-exfiltration/SSRF vector. DELETE is a local record removal (no cascade, no external side effect). The one authority-honesty risk to hold: wiring create/edit must NOT imply or enable EvalRun/scoring/judging — the assessment boundary must stay hard, or the surface would over-claim authority it does not have. Overall risk: LOW (durable local state mutation, no secrets/wallet/irreversibility).
